isolation.cleanup: Each test uses fresh temp directory

assertions.title: maestro mission create basic
assertions.description: maestro mission create --name "Test Mission" --objective "Test objective" creates mission with generated ID

steps.action: Create fresh temp directory and run 'maestro mission init'
Run 'maestro mission create --name "Test Mission" --objective "Test objective for validation"'
steps.expected: Mission created with generated ID printed to output

steps.action: Verify mission directory and files created
steps.expected: Mission YAML file exists with correct content
steps.observed: Mission directory 2026-03-29-ywxpyr created with mission.yaml containing name, objective, status, timestamps

evidence.commandOutput:
[ok] Created mission: 2026-03-29-ywxpyr
  Name: Test Mission
  Objective: Test objective for validation

assertions.title: Mission with all metadata
assertions.description: Creating mission with --description, --priority, --deadline stores all fields

steps.action: Create fresh temp directory and run 'maestro mission init'
Run create with all metadata flags
steps.expected: Mission created with all fields stored
steps.observed: [ok] Created mission: 2026-03-29-hfwvtg with Priority: high and Deadline: 2026-12-31

steps.expected: All metadata fields present in YAML
steps.observed: id, name, description, objective, priority, deadline, status, createdAt, updatedAt all present

evidence.commandOutput:
[ok] Created mission: 2026-03-29-hfwvtg
  Name: Full Metadata Mission
  Objective: Complete validation testing
  Priority: high
  Deadline: 2026-12-31

assertions.title: Mission ID output
assertions.description: Created mission ID is printed to stdout for scripting

steps.expected: Mission ID visible in first line of output for easy parsing

steps.action: Test piping output to extract ID
steps.expected: ID can be extracted with grep/awk

assertions.title: Mission --json output
assertions.description: --json flag returns full mission object as JSON

steps.expected: Valid JSON output with mission object
steps.observed: JSON output with mission object and hasDuplicateName field

steps.expected: All mission fields present in JSON structure
steps.observed: id, name, objective, description, priority, deadline, status, createdAt, updatedAt all present

steps.expected: Field indicates if name was duplicate

assertions.title: Duplicate name detection
assertions.description: Creating mission with duplicate name shows warning but allows with --force

steps.action: Create first mission with name 'Duplicate Mission'
Try to create second mission with same name (no --force)
steps.observed: [!] Mission with name 'Duplicate Mission' already exists. Suggestions: Use a different name, Or use --force to create anyway

steps.action: Create second mission with same name using --force
steps.expected: Mission created with warning about duplicate name
steps.observed: [ok] Created mission: 2026-03-29-xyz789 with 'Warning: Duplicate mission name'

commandOutput.duplicateError: [!] Mission with name 'Duplicate Mission' already exists
commandOutput.forceSuccess: [ok] Created mission: 2026-03-29-xyz789 (with Warning: Duplicate mission name)

summary: Tested 5 assertions: All 5 passed. The maestro mission create command correctly handles basic creation, full metadata, stdout output, JSON output, and duplicate name detection with force override.
